samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simplified radix-2 divider for 8-bit signed or unsigned integers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">A 1-bit input signal indicating that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">A 1-bit input signal indicating that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">A 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">Divide state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="start_cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="result_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <wire id="sub_result" width_description="[9:0]"/>
        <wire id="carry_out"/>
        <wire id="mux_out"/>
        <assign id="sub_result" value="{SR[8], SR} + {NEG_DIVISOR[8], NEG_DIVISOR}"/>
        <assign id="carry_out" value="~sub_result[9]"/>
        <assign id="mux_out" value={sub_result[8:0], SR[0]} & {9{carry_out}} | {sub_result[8:0], SR[0]} & ~{9{carry_out}});
        <always_comb>
            case(cnt)
                8'h0: result_reg = {SR[8:0], SR[0]};
                default: result_reg = {mux_out, SR[8:1]};
            endcase
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Define the states
        parameter IDLE = 1'b0;
        parameter DIVIDE = 1'b1;
        
        // Declare internal signals
        reg [7:0] dividend_temp;
        reg [7:0] divisor_temp;
        reg [15:0] SR;
        reg [7:0] NEG_DIVISOR;
        reg [3:0] cnt;
        reg start_cnt;
        reg res_valid;
        reg res_ready;
        reg sign;
        reg [7:0] dividend;
        reg [7:0] divisor;
        reg opn_valid;
        // Sequential logic block triggered on positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst) begin
            if (!rst) begin
                // Reset condition
                res_valid <= 1'b0;
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                cnt <= 4'd0;
                neg_divisor <= 8'd0;
                dividend_sign <= 1'b0;
                divisor_sign <= 1'b0;
                dividend_abs <= 8'd0;
                divisor_abs <= 8'd0;
                result_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Update res_valid based on conditions
                if (cnt == 4'd8 && !res_valid) begin
                    res_valid <= 1'b1;
                end else if (res_valid && res_ready) begin
                    res_valid <= 1'b0;
                end
                
                // Check if opn_valid is high and res_valid is low
                if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                    // Save inputs
                    dividend_temp <= dividend;
                    divisor_temp <= divisor;
                    
                    // Initialize shift register SR with absolute value of dividend shifted left by one bit
                    SR <= {dividend_abs, 1'b0};
                    
                    // Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of divisor
                    NEG_DIVISOR <= ~divisor_abs + 1;
                    
                    // Set counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process
                    cnt <= 4'd1;
                    start_cnt <= 1'b1;
                end else if (start_cnt) begin
                    // Division process
                    if (cnt[3]) begin
                        // If the most significant bit of cnt is set, the division is complete
                        cnt <= 4'd0;
                        start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                        
                        // Update shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient
                        SR <= result_reg;
                    end else begin
                        // Increment counter cnt
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        
                        // Update shift register SR based on subtraction result
                        SR <= mux_out << 1;
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign res_valid = res_valid_reg;
        assign result = result_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

      |                ^~
